body_txt: This trope involves any code of conduct that artificially constrains the choices available to the protagonist. Often (as with Star Trek's Prime Directive), it restricts or prevents their use of phlebotinum that would wrap up the plot in two seconds otherwise. Conveniently forgotten (or hand waved ) when the plot requires it, but some shows do try to use this as a point of plot drama as the protagonists try to find a way to twist the rules to fit the situation. See also Geas ; Restraining Bolt ; Screw the Rules, I'm Doing What's Right! . Can result in Reed Richards Is Useless and Restricted Rescue Operation . If the party with the Obstructive Code Of Conduct is substantially more powerful than the others involved, it can result in Awakening the Sleeping Giant . See Alien Non-Interference Clause and Mind Over Manners for two common versions of this.
